title: Negative scenario with conflicting evidence and safe degradation
description: The answer must acknowledge conflict or insufficiency instead of overclaiming when the provided evidence disagrees.
query: Does the geometry service expose business endpoints in v1? Answer only if the evidence is decisive.

need_proposal.reason: Need to inspect the conflicting sources before answering safely.

conclusions.content: The current evidence is conflicting, so the safe answer is to abstain from a definitive claim about business endpoints.

llm.answer: The provided evidence is conflicting, so there is not enough evidence to state a definitive business-endpoint design for geometry in v1. One source proposes an admin API while another forbids business endpoints, so the safe answer is to abstain. [S1] [S2]

documents.title: Admin API proposal
documents.text: One design note proposes that the geometry service may expose an admin HTTP API for inspection and recompute. It treats that API as an optional but useful debug surface.

documents.title: No-business-endpoints decision
documents.text: Another decision note states that geometry should have no business endpoints in v1 and should communicate only through Kafka, Qdrant payloads and read models.

documents.title: Unrelated noise
documents.text: This unrelated note discusses CSS cleanup and dashboard colors. filler filler filler filler filler filler filler filler filler filler filler.

citems.content: A design note proposes an optional admin HTTP API for geometry inspection and recompute.
A different decision note forbids business endpoints for geometry in v1 and prefers Kafka plus read-side integration.

summaries.content: The boundary evidence conflicts: one note proposes an admin API while another forbids business endpoints in v1.

forbidden_answer_contains: the geometry service definitely exposes business endpoints
the geometry service definitely has no endpoints
